questions.text: Who was the chairman of the Drafting Committee of the Constitution of India?
questions.explanation: Dr. B.R. Ambedkar chaired the 7-member Drafting Committee, formed on 29 August 1947.

questions.text: Who was the first (temporary) president of the Constituent Assembly?
questions.explanation: Dr. Sachchidananda Sinha, as the oldest member present, chaired the first sitting on 9 December 1946 before Dr. Rajendra Prasad was elected permanent President two days later.
questions.source: Widely repeated in SSC CGL test banks — exact year/paper not independently confirmed

questions.text: In which year did India's Constitution come into force?
questions.explanation: Adopted 26 November 1949, but came into force on 26 January 1950 (Republic Day).
questions.source: Widely repeated in SSC CGL test banks — exact year/paper not independently confirmed

questions.text: Which member of the original Drafting Committee passed away in 1948 and had to be replaced?
questions.explanation: D.P. Khaitan died in 1948 and was replaced by T.T. Krishnamachari. B.L. Mitter was the other replacement (due to ill health), replaced by N. Madhava Rau.
questions.source: Not an official PYQ — anticipated based on common trap pattern (exact-replacement recall)

questions.text: How many total sittings did the Constituent Assembly hold while drafting the Constitution?
questions.explanation: The Assembly held 11 sessions over 165 sitting days, taking 2 years, 11 months, 18 days in total.
questions.source: Not an official PYQ — anticipated based on common numeric-recall pattern

questions.text: Who was appointed as the Constitutional Advisor to the Constituent Assembly, and prepared the original draft before the Drafting Committee took over?
questions.explanation: Sir B.N. Rau, a civil servant and constitutional expert, prepared the preliminary draft. He's often overshadowed by Ambedkar in students' memory, but is a distinct, frequently-tested name.
questions.source: Not an official PYQ — flagged because this name is easy to overlook and easy to test

questions.text: How many women members were part of the Constituent Assembly?
questions.explanation: 15 women members were part of the Constituent Assembly, including Sarojini Naidu, Hansa Mehta, and Durgabai Deshmukh.
questions.source: Not an official PYQ — an underused but plausible recall fact

questions.text: On which date was the Constitution of India adopted by the Constituent Assembly?
questions.explanation: Adopted on 26 November 1949 (Constitution Day); came into force on 26 January 1950 (Republic Day). This adoption-vs-enforcement date pair is one of the most recurring question patterns in this topic.
questions.source: Pattern frequently recurring across SSC CGL/CHSL polity papers in various phrasings
